Package sample.nb.ant.zenoh

Class ZenohJavaScoutAnt

java.lang.Object
sample.nb.ant.zenoh.ZenohJavaScoutAnt
public class ZenohJavaScoutAnt extends Object
Minimal command-line Zenoh scouting / node-discovery tool built on the pure-Java PureJavaZenohScout facade.

Joins the Zenoh multicast group (default 224.0.0.224:7446), listens for HELLO frames broadcast by nearby routers and peers, and optionally emits SCOUT frames to prompt silent nodes into replying. Prints one line per discovery event (discover / update / expire) to stdout and shuts down cleanly on Ctrl-C or after an optional timeout.

Unlike ZenohJavaPubAnt and ZenohJavaSubAnt this tool never opens a TCP/TLS session to a router. It is a pure UDP-multicast observer that decodes HELLOs and (in active mode) emits SCOUTs. Routers do not see it as a Zenoh peer, client, or session.

Positional arguments (all optional, in order):

  1. mode — active or passive. Default active. active listens AND emits a SCOUT every interval milliseconds; passive only listens for nodes that self-advertise on their own.
  2. interval — active-mode SCOUT interval in milliseconds. Default 3000. Ignored in passive mode. Minimum 250.
  3. roles — comma-separated filter of roles to accept: any of router, peer, client. Default router,peer,client (all three).
  4. timeoutSeconds — optional auto-shutdown timer in seconds. Default 0 = run until Ctrl-C.

Pairs with any running Zenoh router on the same L2 segment for a quick end-to-end smoke test.
